items.title: EPA to Issue Two New PFAS Rules Impacting Drinking Water
items.abstract: The Environmental Protection Agency will issue two new rules aimed at delaying and rescinding maximum contaminant levels in drinking water. The first one will repeal 2024 regulations that set nationwide limits on four per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) – GenX, PFNA, PFBS,

items.title: Forever Chemicals and the Family Farm: What NC Growers Need to Know About PFAS on Agricultural Land
items.abstract: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances, commonly known as PFAS or “forever chemicals,” are a large family of synthetic compounds that have been used since the 1940s in everything from nonstick cookware and food packaging to firefighting foam and industrial coatings....By: Ward and S

items.title: Michigan AG Secures Monsanto PCB Settlement
items.abstract: Michigan AG Dana Nessel secured a settlement with Monsanto Company, Solutia Inc., and Pharmacia LLC to resolve allegations that the companies’ PCB-containing products caused environmental contamination in Michigan....By: Cozen O'Connor

items.title: Microplastics In Drinking Water: What EPA’s Draft CCL 6 Means For The Consumer Products Industry
items.abstract: Overview -  EPA’s April 2026 draft Sixth Contaminant Candidate List (CCL 6) places microplastics on the federal regulatory radar for the first time, a milestone that consumer products companies cannot afford to ignore....By: Integral Consulting Inc.

items.title: Microplastics Move into the Federal Policy Pipeline: What Plastics and Chemicals Companies Need to Know Now

items.title: From Liability to Legacy: Leveraging CERCLA to Protect Public Water Systems
items.abstract: In April 2024, the U.S. EPA finalized National Primary Drinking Water Regulations, setting enforceable Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCLs) for six PFAS, including a stringent 4.0 parts per trillion (ppt) for PFOA and PFOS....By: Napoli Shkolnik
